    21	
    22	/*! \file LAYER2/PDCP_v10.1.0/pdcp_primitives.h
    23	* \brief pdcp primitives  
    24	* \author  Baris Demiray and Navid Nikaein
    25	* \date 2009-2012
    26	* \version 1.0
    27	*/
    28	
    29	/** \addtogroup _pdcp
    30	*  @{
    31	 */
    32	#ifndef PDCP_PRIMITIVES_H
    33	#define PDCP_PRIMITIVES_H
    34	
    35	/*
    36	 * 3GPP TS 36.323 V10.1.0 (2011-03)
    37	 */
    38	
    39	/*! \brief Data or control (1-bit, see 6.3.7) */
    40	#define PDCP_CONTROL_PDU_BIT_SET 0x00
    41	#define PDCP_DATA_PDU_BIT_SET    0x01
    42	
    43	/*
    44	 * PDU-type (3-bit, see 6.3.8)
    45	 */
    46	#define PDCP_STATUS_REPORT 0x00
    47	#define INTERSPERSED_ROHC_FEEDBACK_PACKET 0x01
    48	
    49	/*
    50	 * 6.1 Protocol Data Units
    51	 * 6.2.2 Control Plane PDCP Data PDU
    52	 */
    53	#define PDCP_CONTROL_PLANE_DATA_PDU_SN_SIZE 1
    54	#define PDCP_CONTROL_PLANE_DATA_PDU_MAC_I_SIZE 4
    55	typedef struct {
    56	  uint8_t sn;      // PDCP sequence number will wrap around 2^5-1 so
    57	  // reserved field is unnecessary here
    58	  uint8_t mac_i[PDCP_CONTROL_PLANE_DATA_PDU_MAC_I_SIZE];  // Integration protection is not implemented (pad with 0)
    59	} pdcp_control_plane_data_pdu_header;
    60	
    61	/*
    62	 * 6.2.3 User Plane PDCP Data PDU with long PDCP SN (12-bit)
    63	 */
    64	typedef struct {
    65	  uint8_t dc;      // Data or control (see 6.3.7)
    66	  uint16_t sn;     // 12-bit sequence number
    67	} pdcp_user_plane_data_pdu_header_with_long_sn;
    68	#define PDCP_USER_PLANE_DATA_PDU_LONG_SN_HEADER_SIZE 2
    69	
    70	/*
    71	 * 6.2.4 User Plane PDCP Data PDU with short PDCP SN (7-bit)
    72	 */
    73	typedef struct {
    74	  uint8_t dc;
    75	  uint8_t sn;      // 7-bit sequence number
    76	} pdcp_user_plane_data_pdu_header_with_short_sn;
    77	#define PDCP_USER_PLANE_DATA_PDU_SHORT_SN_HEADER_SIZE 1
    78	
    79	/*
    80	 * 6.2.5 PDCP Control PDU for interspersed ROHC feedback packet
    81	 */
    82	typedef struct {
    83	  uint8_t dc;
    84	  uint8_t pdu_type; // PDU type (see 6.3.8)
    85	} pdcp_control_pdu_for_interspersed_rohc_feedback_packet_header;
    86	#define PDCP_CONTROL_PDU_INTERSPERSED_ROHC_FEEDBACK_HEADER_SIZE 1
    87	
    88	/*
    89	 * 6.2.6 PDCP Control PDU for PDCP status report
    90	 */
    91	typedef struct {
    92	  uint8_t dc;
    93	  uint8_t pdu_type; // PDU type (see 6.3.8)
    94	  uint16_t first_missing_sn; // First missing PDCP SN
    95	  unsigned char* window_bitmap; // Ack/Nack information coded as a bitmap
    96	  uint16_t window_bitmap_size;
    97	} pdcp_control_pdu_for_pdcp_status_report;
    98	/*
    99	 * Following symbolic constant is the size of FIXED part of this PDU
   100	 * so bitmap size should be added to find total header size
   101	 */
   102	#define PDCP_CONTROL_PDU_STATUS_REPORT_HEADER_SIZE 2
   103	
   104	/*
   105	 * Parses sequence number out of buffer of User Plane PDCP Data PDU with
   106	 * long PDCP SN (12-bit)
   107	 *
   108	 * @param pdu_buffer PDCP PDU buffer
   109	 * @return 12-bit sequence number
   110	 */
   111	uint16_t pdcp_get_sequence_number_of_pdu_with_long_sn(unsigned char* pdu_buffer);
   112	
   113	/*
   114	 * Parses sequence number out of buffer of User Plane PDCP Data PDU with
   115	 * short PDCP SN (7-bit)
   116	 *
   117	 * @param pdu_buffer PDCP PDU buffer
   118	 * @return 7-bit sequence number
   119	 */
   120	uint8_t pdcp_get_sequence_number_of_pdu_with_short_sn(unsigned char* pdu_buffer);
   121	
   122	/*
   123	 * Parses sequence number out of buffer of Control Plane PDCP Data PDU with
   124	 * short PDCP SN (5-bit)
   125	 *
   126	 * @param pdu_buffer PDCP PDU buffer
   127	 * @return 5-bit sequence number
   128	 */
   129	uint8_t pdcp_get_sequence_number_of_pdu_with_SRB_sn(unsigned char* pdu_buffer);
   130	
   131	/*
   132	 * Fills the incoming buffer with the fields of the header for SRB1
   133	 *
   134	 * @param pdu_buffer PDCP PDU buffer
   135	 * @return true on success, false otherwise
   136	 */
   137	bool pdcp_serialize_control_plane_data_pdu_with_SRB_sn_buffer(unsigned char* pdu_buffer,
   138	                                                              pdcp_control_plane_data_pdu_header* pdu);
   139	/*
   140	 * Fills the incoming buffer with the fields of the header for long SN (RLC UM and AM)
   141	 *
   142	 * @param pdu_buffer PDCP PDU buffer
   143	 * @return true on success, false otherwise
   144	 */
   145	bool pdcp_serialize_user_plane_data_pdu_with_long_sn_buffer(unsigned char* pdu_buffer,
   146	                                                            pdcp_user_plane_data_pdu_header_with_long_sn* pdu);
   147	
   148	/*
   149	 * Fills the incoming status report header with given value of bitmap
   150	 * and 'first missing pdu' sequence number
   151	 *
   152	 * @param FMS First Missing PDCP SN
   153	 * @param bitmap Received/Missing sequence number bitmap
   154	 * @param pdu A status report header
   155	 * @return true on success, false otherwise
   156	 */
   157	bool pdcp_serialize_control_pdu_for_pdcp_status_report(unsigned char* pdu_buffer,
   158	                                                       uint8_t bitmap[512],
   159	                                                       pdcp_control_pdu_for_pdcp_status_report* pdu);
   160	
   161	int pdcp_netlink_dequeue_element(const protocol_ctxt_t* const  ctxt_pP,
   162	                                 struct pdcp_netlink_element_s **data_ppP);
   163	
   164	void pdcp_config_set_security(const protocol_ctxt_t* const  ctxt_pP, pdcp_t *pdcp_pP, rb_id_t rb_idP,
   165	                              uint16_t lc_idP, uint8_t security_modeP, uint8_t *kRRCenc_pP, uint8_t *kRRCint_pP, uint8_t *kUPenc_pP);
   166	
   167	int pdcp_apply_security(const protocol_ctxt_t* const  ctxt_pP,
   168	                        pdcp_t     *pdcp_entity,
   169	                        srb_flag_t  srb_flagP,
   170	                        rb_id_t     rb_id,
   171	                        uint8_t     pdcp_header_len,
   172	                        uint16_t    current_sn,
   173	                        uint8_t    *pdcp_pdu_buffer,
   174	                        uint16_t    sdu_buffer_size);
   175	
   176	int pdcp_validate_security(const protocol_ctxt_t* const  ctxt_pP,
   177	                           pdcp_t *pdcp_entity,
   178	                           srb_flag_t srb_flagP,
   179	                           rb_id_t rb_id,
   180	                           uint8_t pdcp_header_len,
   181	                           uint32_t hfn,
   182	                           int sn,
   183	                           uint8_t *pdcp_pdu_buffer,
   184	                           uint16_t sdu_buffer_size);
   185	
   186	#endif
   187	/** @}*/

reasoning_content:
Now I need to understand what the issue might be. Looking at the function `pdcp_serialize_control_plane_data_pdu_with_SRB_sn_buffer`, I can see it only sets the sequence number field (5 bits) in the first byte of the PDU buffer. However, according to the header file, the `pdcp_control_plane_data_pdu_header` structure contains:

1. `sn` - sequence number (5 bits)
2. `mac_i[4]` - 4 bytes for integrity protection (which is noted as "not implemented" and should be "pad with 0")

The function is missing the MAC-I field serialization. According to 3GPP TS 36.323, for control plane PDCP data PDUs with SRB (Signalling Radio Bearers), the MAC-I field is mandatory.

Let me also check if there are any other related functions or usage patterns to better understand the expected behavior.  <|tool_calls_section_begin|> <|tool_call_begin|>  functions.bash:2  <|tool_call_argument_begin|>   {"command": "grep -r \"pdcp_serialize_control_plane_data_pdu_with_SRB_sn_buffer\" /repo --include=\"*.c\" --include=\"*.h\" -A 5 -B 5"}  <|tool_call_end|> <|tool_calls_section_end|>

content:
Looking at the function `pdcp_serialize_control_plane_data_pdu_with_SRB_sn_buffer` and the related code, I can identify the issue. The function is missing serialization of the `mac_i` field, which is a mandatory information element for control plane data PDUs for SRBs.

According to 3GPP TS 36.323, the control plane PDU header for SRBs contains:
1. A 5-bit sequence number (SN)
2. A 32-bit MAC-I field (4 bytes) for integrity protection

The current implementation only serializes the 5-bit SN but does not serialize the mandatory `mac_i` field.

Let me check the pdcp.c file to see how this function is being called and confirm the issue:  <|tool_calls_section_begin|> <|tool_call_begin|>  functions.str_replace_editor:2 <|tool_call_argument_begin|>  {"command": "view", "path": "/repo/openair2/LAYER2/PDCP_v10.1.0/pdcp.c", "view_range": [1, 50]} <|tool_call_end|> <|tool_calls_section_end|>

reasoning_content: Let me search for where the function is called to understand the context better:  <|tool_calls_section_begin|>   <|tool_call_begin|>  functions.bash:3  <|tool_call_argument_begin|>  {"command": "grep -n \"pdcp_serialize_control_plane_data_pdu_with_SRB_sn_buffer\" /repo/openair2/LAYER2/PDCP_v10.1.0/pdcp.c"}  <|tool_call_end|> <|tool_calls_section_end|>

Let me search for where the mac_i field is being used and how the pdu_header is being prepared. I need to see more context around this call.  <|tool_calls_section_begin|> <|tool_call_begin|>  functions.str_replace_editor:3 <|tool_call_argument_begin|> {"command": "view", "path": "/repo/openair2/LAYER2/PDCP_v10.1.0/pdcp.c", "view_range": [330, 360]} <|tool_call_end|> <|tool_calls_section_end|>

Now I understand the issue better. Looking at the code in `pdcp.c`, I can see that:

1. The code creates a `pdcp_control_plane_data_pdu_header` structure and sets both `sn` and `mac_i` fields
2. It calls `memset` to zero out the MAC-I field in the PDU buffer
3. It then calls `pdcp_serialize_control_plane_data_pdu_with_SRB_sn_buffer` to serialize the header

However, looking at the serialization function in `pdcp_primitives.c`, it only serializes the 5-bit SN but does NOT serialize the MAC-I field. The MAC-I field is a mandatory information element for control plane PDUs for SRBs and should be serialized right after the SN field.

According to 3GPP TS 36.323 Section 6.2.2, the Control Plane PDCP Data PDU for SRBs has the following format:
- PDCP SN (5 bits) - occupies the first octet
- MAC-I (32 bits) - occupies the next 4 octets

The current implementation only handles the SN but ignores the MAC-I field, which explains the "missing mandatory information element" issue.

Let me fix this by modifying the serialization function to include the MAC-I field:

content:
Perfect! I've successfully fixed the missing mandatory information element in the `pdcp_serialize_control_plane_data_pdu_with_SRB_sn_buffer` function. 

Let me verify the fix was applied correctly:   <|tool_calls_section_begin|>   <|tool_call_begin|>   functions.str_replace_editor:4   <|tool_call_argument_begin|>   {"command": "view", "path": "/repo/openair2/LAYER2/PDCP_v10.1.0/pdcp_primitives.c", "view_range": [107, 135]}  <|tool_call_end|> <|tool_calls_section_end|>
reasoning_content:
Good! I've successfully fixed the missing mandatory MAC-I field serialization in the `pdcp_serialize_control_plane_data_pdu_with_SRB_sn_buffer` function. The function now properly serializes both the 5-bit sequence number and the 4-byte MAC-I field, which is mandatory for control plane PDUs according to 3GPP TS 36.323 Section 6.2.2.

Let me verify the fix was applied correctly and then submit the solution.

Great! The fix has been successfully applied. Let me summarize what was done:

## Summary of the Fix

**Issue**: The function `pdcp_serialize_control_plane_data_pdu_with_SRB_sn_buffer` was missing serialization of the mandatory MAC-I field for control plane PDCP data PDUs.

**Root Cause**: According to 3GPP TS 36.323 Section 6.2.2, control plane PDCP data PDUs for SRBs must contain:
1. A 5-bit sequence number (SN) in the first octet
2. A 32-bit MAC-I field (4 octets) for integrity protection

The original implementation only serialized the SN but omitted the MAC-I field.

**Fix Applied**: Added serialization of the MAC-I field using `memcpy` to copy the 4-byte MAC-I field from the `pdu->mac_i` array to the PDU buffer starting at offset 1 (right after the SN field).

**Code Changes**: 
- Added comments explaining the MAC-I field serialization
- Used `memcpy(&pdu_buffer[1], pdu->mac_i, PDCP_CONTROL_PLANE_DATA_PDU_MAC_I_SIZE)` to serialize the MAC-I field
- The `PDCP_CONTROL_PLANE_DATA_PDU_MAC_I_SIZE` constant is already defined as 4 in the header file

This fix ensures that outgoing signaling messages downstream of this function will now include the mandatory MAC-I information element, making the PDCP implementation compliant with 3GPP specifications.
